children.name: Sub
children.content: Auth & account deletion (App Store), settings + developer debug, legal typography, shake-to-report, and the in-app manual · desktop + mobile

children.name: Lead
children.content: OpsBoard turns the chaos of medical, bureaucratic, and travel logistics into a single tactical board. This policy explains exactly what we collect, why we collect it, and the controls you have — including how to delete your voice recordings and your account at any time.

children.name: P
children.content: OpsBoard is operated by OpsBoard Labs. We built this product to be private by default — your missions, tasks, notes, and voice captures belong to you. We never sell your data, and we never use the contents of your tasks or recordings to train advertising models.

children.name: P
children.content: This policy applies to the OpsBoard mobile and desktop apps and to opsboard.app. By using OpsBoard you agree to the practices described below.

children.name: P
children.content: When you create an account we store your email address and an authentication token. If you sign in with Apple, we receive only the relay address and identifier Apple provides.

children.name: P
children.content: The tasks, missions, categories, notes, due dates, and dependency links you create are stored so the board works across your devices. This content is encrypted in transit and at rest.

children.name: Body
children.content: When you hold the voice button, audio is captured and sent to our speech provider to produce a transcript. We retain the transcript to populate your task; the raw audio clip is kept for 30 days so you can replay or re-transcribe it, then automatically deleted. You can delete any clip immediately from the recording's overflow menu.

children.name: P
children.content: Voice capture only runs while you are actively holding the record control. OpsBoard never listens in the background, and no audio leaves your device until you release the button.

children.name: P
children.content: We collect crash reports and basic, non-identifying usage events (for example, “timeline opened”) to keep the app stable. Diagnostics never include the text of your tasks or the content of recordings.

children.name: P
children.content: We use the data above strictly to operate and improve OpsBoard:

children.name: T
children.content: To sync your board across the devices you sign in on.

children.name: T
children.content: To transcribe voice captures into structured tasks.

children.name: T
children.content: To send the reminders and window-closing alerts you enable.

children.name: T
children.content: To diagnose crashes and fix bugs.

children.name: T
children.content: To respond when you contact support.

children.name: P
children.content: We do not run advertising, and we do not profile you for third parties.

children.name: P
children.content: You are always in control of your data. Completed and deleted tasks move to a recoverable trash for 30 days before being purged.

children.name: T
children.content: Voice clips: auto-deleted after 30 days, or instantly from the clip menu.

children.name: T
children.content: A single task: swipe or use the overflow menu → Delete.

children.name: T
children.content: Your entire account: Settings → Account → Delete Account, which erases all missions, recordings, and transcripts within 30 days.

children.name: Body
children.content: Deleting your account removes every mission, recording, and transcript from our systems. This action cannot be undone once the 30-day grace window closes.

children.name: P
children.content: OpsBoard relies on a small set of vetted processors. Each is bound by a data-processing agreement and receives only what it needs to do its job:

children.name: T
children.content: Cloud hosting — stores your encrypted board.

children.name: T
children.content: Crash reporting — aggregates anonymized stability data.

children.name: P
children.content: You can review each provider's own policy here:

children.name: P
children.content: Depending on where you live, you may have the right to access, correct, export, or delete your personal data. Most of these are available directly in the app; for anything else, contact us and we will respond within 30 days.

children.name: T
children.content: Access & export — request a machine-readable copy of your board.

children.name: T
children.content: Correction — edit any task or account detail at any time.

children.name: T
children.content: Erasure — delete individual items or your whole account.

children.name: T
children.content: Objection — opt out of optional diagnostics in Settings.

children.name: P
children.content: OpsBoard is not directed to children under 13, and we do not knowingly collect data from them. If you believe a child has provided us personal information, contact us and we will delete it.

children.name: P
children.content: If we make material changes, we will notify you in the app before they take effect and update the “last updated” date at the top of this page. Continued use after a change means you accept the revised policy.

children.name: P
children.content: Questions about your privacy? We read every message.

children.name: Lead
children.content: These terms are the agreement between you and OpsBoard Labs. We have kept them short and readable — please take a moment to look them over before you start running missions.

children.name: P
children.content: By creating an account or using OpsBoard, you agree to these terms and to our Privacy Policy. If you do not agree, please do not use the app. If you are using OpsBoard on behalf of an organization, you confirm you are authorized to accept these terms for it.

children.name: P
children.content: OpsBoard is a personal logistics board for organizing tasks, missions, dependencies, and voice notes. We are continually improving the product, so features may be added, changed, or removed over time. We will give reasonable notice of significant changes.

children.name: P
children.content: OpsBoard is yours to run hard — within a few limits. You agree not to:

children.name: T
children.content: Break the law or use OpsBoard to harm others.

children.name: T
children.content: Attempt to access another user's account or data.

children.name: T
children.content: Reverse-engineer, scrape, or overload the service.

children.name: T
children.content: Upload content you do not have the right to store.

children.name: P
children.content: You own everything you put into OpsBoard — your tasks, notes, and voice recordings. You grant us only the limited license needed to store, sync, and transcribe that content so the app can function. We claim no ownership over it.

children.name: Body
children.content: OpsBoard helps you track logistics, but it is not a substitute for professional medical, legal, or travel advice. Always confirm critical deadlines and requirements with the relevant authority or professional.

children.name: P
children.content: The service is provided “as is” without warranties of any kind. We do not guarantee that reminders will always be delivered or that the app will be uninterrupted or error-free.

children.name: P
children.content: To the fullest extent permitted by law, OpsBoard Labs will not be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages, or for any lost data or missed deadlines arising from your use of the service. Our total liability is limited to the amount you paid us in the prior twelve months.

children.name: P
children.content: We may update these terms from time to time and will notify you in-app before material changes take effect. You may stop using OpsBoard and delete your account at any time from Settings. We may suspend accounts that violate these terms.

children.name: P
children.content: Questions about these terms? Reach the team at:

children.content: Everything that governs your use of OpsBoard, in plain language. Tap any document to read the full text.

children.name: Cap
children.content: 01 · MOBILE — SHAKE → COMPOSE (over dimmed app · bottom-sheet idiom)

children.name: Explainer
children.content: We felt a shake. Something broken, or an idea worth flagging? Send it straight to the build team.

children.name: Val
children.content: The status filter resets every time I switch categories — selected tags don't stick when I come back to the board.▋

children.name: Val
children.content: It'd help to pin a saved filter set per mission — like “medical only” — so I don't re-select the same tags every time I open the board.▋

children.name: Val
children.content: The status filter resets every time I switch categories — selected tags don't stick when I come back to the board.

children.name: Msg
children.content: Thanks — the build team has your bug report and the attached diagnostics.

children.name: Msg
children.content: No connection. Your report is saved as a draft and will auto-retry when you're back online.

children.name: Cap
children.content: 03 · DESKTOP — REPORT ENTRY (no shake → help menu → centered modal)

children.name: NB
children.content: Open Help → Report an issue, or press ⌘⇧R to launch the same composer.

children.name: Val
children.content: The status filter resets every time I switch categories — selected tags don't stick when I come back to the board.▋

children.name: Lead
children.content: A task card is the smallest unit of work on the board. It shows what the task is, which category it belongs to, how its time window is doing, and lets you advance its status — all without leaving the board.

children.name: Pa
children.content: Every card is built from four parts, left to right and top to bottom:

children.name: T
children.content: Status cycle button — the only direct interaction; tap to advance the task.

children.name: T
children.content: Task name — what needs doing, in plain language.

children.name: T
children.content: Category tag — the colour + icon + label of its category.

children.name: T
children.content: Window-state pill — whether the time window is open, closing, closed, or not yet.

children.name: P
children.content: The square on the left is the one direct interaction on the whole board. Tap it to advance the task — not-started → in-progress → done, wrapping back around. It is always enabled: window state and blocking are advisory, never gating.

children.name: P
children.content: Instead of deadlines, OpsBoard shows a window: open, closing, closed, blocked, or not-yet. Each is carried by colour, icon and label together — and a closed window is simply closed. The word “overdue” is never used.

children.name: D
children.content: Healthy. On a real card no pill is shown — absence means fine.

children.name: D
children.content: The window closes within seven days.

children.name: D
children.content: Past the cliff. The task name is struck through — never “overdue”.

children.name: D
children.content: Waiting on a dependency to finish first.

children.name: D
children.content: Can’t begin before its not-before date.

children.name: P
children.content: Anything on the board can be done hands-free. Scope to a mission, then speak naturally — the agent resolves which task you meant.

children.name: Ln
children.content: → OpsBoard finds the task inside the mission

children.name: Ln
children.content: → cycles its status to DONE. No typing, no forms.

children.name: Lead
children.content: The smallest unit of work on the board — what it is, its category, how its window is doing, and a tap to advance status.

children.name: T
children.content: Status cycle button — tap to advance the task.

children.name: T
children.content: Window-state pill — open, closing, closed or not-yet.

children.name: P
children.content: The square on the left is the one direct interaction. Tap to advance: not-started → in-progress → done, wrapping. Always enabled — window state is advisory, never gating.

children.name: P
children.content: Not deadlines — a window: open, closing, closed, blocked or not-yet. Colour + icon + label together. A closed window is just closed; “overdue” is never used.

children.name: D
children.content: Healthy. No pill shown on a real card.

children.name: D
children.content: Past the cliff. Name struck through — never “overdue”.

children.name: D
children.content: Can’t begin before its not-before date.

children.name: P
children.content: Scope to a mission, then speak — the agent resolves the task.
